docs.title: Ender 3 CNC Build Manual
docs.text:
Welcome to the Ender 3 CNC build manual. This guide walks you through converting an Ender 3 3D printer into a fully functional CNC machine.

⚠️ Note: This guide may contain errors. If you find any issues with hardware or instructions, please open an issue in the repository.

Use the sidebar to navigate chapters.

docs.title: Belts and Spindle
docs.text: This chapter covers routing the GT2 belts.

docs.title: 1. Parts Required
docs.text: Qty Item Source Notes 3pc ~500mm GT2 6mm Belt Buy Measure carefully before cutting 12pc M3x10 Buy Some may be spare from Ender3 6pc Printed Belt Clamp Printed Holds belt ends securely

docs.title: 2. Belt Installation
docs.text:
1. Route GT2 belts along X and Y axes.
  2. Use printed belt clamps to secure belt ends.
  3. Ensure belts are tight but not overly stretched.
  4. Test X/Y motion manually to confirm smooth travel.

docs.title: Firmware Setup
docs.text: This chapter explains firmware options for your Ender 3 PRO CNC conversion.

docs.title: 1. Options
docs.text: Firmware Notes Klipper Recommended if using Raspberry Pi or similar GRBL Standard CNC option, works with ESP32/32-bit boards

docs.title: 2. Klipper Setup
docs.text:
1. Follow standard Klipper installation on your Raspberry Pi or Linux laptop.
  2. Copy a reference printer.cfg for the Ender CNC:
  3. Set stepper, endstop, spindle, and belt parameters.
  4. Test all axes manually before enabling motor power.
  5. Configure homing and probe offsets for your spindle.

docs.title: 3. GRBL Setup
docs.text:
1. Flash your GRBL-compatible board according to manufacturer instructions.
  2. Use the reference configuration for stepper settings, endstops, and spindle control.
  3. Test movement manually before running any G-Code.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Tip

Keep a backup of working firmware configuration before making major changes.

Warning

Never run motors at full speed during first test — verify wiring and mechanical alignment first.

docs.title: Frame Assembly
docs.text: This chapter walks through building the Ender 3 PRO CNC frame, mounting XY joints, and preparing for the X+Z carriage assembly.

docs.title: 1. Parts Required
docs.text: Qty Item Source Notes 48pc M5x16 BHSC Buy Frame bolts 4pc M5x40 Ender3 Z-axis attachment 4pc M5x8 Ender3 Extra spacers 4pc M5 1mm Shims Buy For precise leveling 52pc M5 T-nuts Buy Frame assembly

docs.title: 2. Safety Notes
docs.text:
Warning

Do not press in the endstops until wiring is complete. They are press-fit and may be difficult to remove afterward.

Tip

Use a flat surface when squaring the frame for best results.

docs.title: 3. Assemble the Frame
docs.text:
1. Lay out the aluminum extrusions according to the frame diagram.
  2. Take note of blind joints and the 20mm offset on the back extrusion.
  3. Loosely attach M5 bolts and T-nuts to hold the frame together.
  4. Check that the frame is square:
  5. Measure diagonals; they should be equal.
  6. Adjust joints as necessary.

docs.title: Parts Required
docs.text: Qty Item Source Notes 8pc M5x45 SHSC Ender3 Remove shims 8pc M3x10 Buy 8pc 8mm Spacer (printed or 5mm bore alu) Buy Do not use Ender3 aluminum spacers here

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Assemble XY joints without pressing in the endstop.
  2. Loosely attach 20T pulleys.
  3. Mount XY joints to the frame:
  4. Slide bottom wheels into V-slot and loosely add M5 nuts.
  5. Add top wheels with a slight wiggle to pop them into place.
  6. Adjust angles carefully.
  7. Double-check distance between X extrusion mounting points before cutting the X extrusion to length.

docs.title: 5. Cut X Extrusion
docs.text:
1. Measure your frame; Ender 3 frames may vary ± a few mm.
  2. Cut X extrusion to size.
  3. Tap 4 new M5 threads on the cut side.

Tip

If you are unsure, measure twice before cutting. Once cut, you cannot undo it.

docs.title: 6. Final Checks
docs.text:
• Ensure frame is square and rigid.
  • Make sure XY joints move smoothly by hand.
  • Endstops are still unpressed for now.

After completing these steps, your frame is ready for the X+Z carriage assembly.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
This is a reference BOM from my own build and CAD. I might have counted wrong. If you find any issues please let me know.

I've tried to keep the BOM parts as close as possible to a Voron 3D Printer build in hopes people already have some of the BOM already.

docs.title: !NOTE! Some Ender 3's might be sligthly different in what hardware was used in what year/make. So please dont follow it 100% blindly.
You can re-use 3 limit switches from the Ender 3, but you need to cut them off the PCB's and you'll still be missing 1, so you need to order AT LEAST 1 more.
You can re-use 2 belt lengts from the Ender 3, but you still need one more ~500mm lenght, so better to just buy get 2-3 meters to have some spare too.
docs.text: Quantity Part Description Notes M5 Screws 10x M5x30 button head 16x M5x45 bolts From E3P frame (remove shims if possible) 4x M5x8 1x M5x65 Middle lower X axis wheel (optional) All M5 nuts From your Ender 3 M4 Screws 2x M4x20 2x M4x16 2x M4x10 Includes 2x M4 t-nuts M3 Screws 6x M3x10 11x M3x10 Additional 4x M3x40 12x M3x6 Bearings 2x 688 bearings If your E3P has these; otherwise buy 608ZZ

docs.title: Parts to Purchase
docs.text: Quantity Part Description Specifications Notes Motion Components 3x GT2 belts ~500mm length each, 6mm width Buy 3-5 meter roll on AliExpress 3x GT2 pulleys 20T, 6mm width, 5mm bore 2x MGN12H linear rails 150mm length 1 carriage per rail 2x 608ZZ bearings 8x22x7mm Or use 688 from Ender 3 Electronics 4x D2F-L Microswitch Limit switch Get 10 pack on AliExpress Fasteners ~20x M3 T-nuts For 2020 extrusion Sliding or standard t-nuts ~40x M5 T-nuts For 2020 extrusion Sliding or standard t-nuts 10x M5 locknuts Hex nuts ~70x M5x16 button head screws 22x M3x10 screws Not countersunk 12x M3x8 SHCS Socket head cap screws Hardware 30x Heat set inserts M3x4x5mm Voron spec 4x M5 shims OD 10mm, ID 5mm, 1mm thick

docs.title: Optional Upgrades
docs.text: Quantity Part Description Specifications Purpose 8x Aluminum spacers 5mm ID x 8mm OD x 8mm length Replaces printed Y axis wheel spacers

docs.title: 3D Printed Parts
docs.text:
• PLA+, 5 walls, 5 top/bottom layers, 40% gyroid infill
  • Press-fit pulleys, spacers, and clamps

Print orientation to avoid supports:

docs.title: Dremel Clamp
docs.text: This chapter covers attaching your spindle or Dremel clamp.

docs.title: 1. Parts Required
docs.text: Qty Item Source Notes 4pc M5x10 Buy Some may be spare from Ender3 1pc Dremel or Ø52–65mm Clamp Buy/Printed Depends on spindle choice

docs.title: 2. Spindle / Dremel Clamp Installation
docs.text:
1. Choose the clamp compatible with your spindle/Dremel.
  2. Attach the clamp to the X carriage plate using M3 or M5 screws as per your design.
  3. Slide spindle or Dremel into the clamp.
  4. Adjust the height and secure tightly.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Tip

If using a Dremel, you may need to slightly modify the printed clamp to fit snugly.

Warning

Ensure the spindle cannot move during operation — any slip can damage the machine or workpiece.

docs.title: Tools
docs.text:
Tool Notes M5 Tap For cutting threads in aluminum extrusions Hex Drives Standard metric hex keys Dremel or spindle Check compatibility with clamps Printed press-fit pulley extraction tool Optional: can cut pulleys if unavailable Saw To cut the aluminum extrusion

💡 Tip: Use a flat surface for squaring the frame.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Common issues and fixes during your Ender 3 PRO CNC build.

docs.title: 1. Frame Issues
docs.text:
• Frame not square: Adjust diagonal measurements.
  • XY joints binding: Loosen wheels slightly and re-align.

docs.title: 2. Carriage & Z-axis Issues
docs.text:
• Z carriage binds: Check MGN12H rail alignment.
  • Leadscrew doesn’t turn smoothly: Verify coupler alignment and thread engagement.

docs.title: 3. Belts & Spindle
docs.text:
• Belts slipping: Retighten or replace clamps.
  • Spindle not secure: Adjust printed clamp or use alternative spacer.

docs.title: 4. Wiring & Electronics
docs.text:
• Limit switch not triggered: Check wiring and press-fit alignment.
  • Motors not moving: Verify connections and firmware configuration.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Tip

Test movement manually before applying power or running G-Code.

Warning

Always disconnect PSU before making wiring adjustments.

docs.title: Wiring and Electronics
docs.text: This chapter covers connecting limit switches, wiring the motors, and PSU setup.

docs.title: 1. Parts Required
docs.text: Qty Item Source Notes 4pc Limit Switches Buy Endstops for X/Y/Z Wires As needed Buy JST connectors optional 1pc Electronics Enclosure Buy/Print Fits Raspberry Pi / SKR board / SSR PSU Power Supply Buy Ensure safe handling

docs.title: 2. Limit Switch Installation
docs.text:
1. Solder wires to all 4 limit switches.
  2. Insert switches into the press-fit slots in the frame.
  3. Test that switches sit securely:
  4. If too tight → lightly sand faces.
  5. If too loose → add shims or a small amount of hot glue.
  6. Leave switches unpressed until the frame and carriage are fully installed.

docs.title: 3. Wire Management
docs.text:
1. Use zip-tie mounts for clean wire routing.
  2. Keep wires away from moving parts.
  3. Route motor, limit switch, and spindle wires neatly to the electronics enclosure.

docs.title: 4. Electronics Enclosure
docs.text:
1. Choose enclosure based on your board (Raspberry Pi, SKR Pico, 4.2.2, 4.2.7).
  2. Modify enclosure if necessary to fit additional components like SSR.
  3. PSU installation:
  4. Only connect once wiring is fully complete.
  5. Practice safety and follow electrical guidelines.

docs.title: Warnings
docs.text:
Warning

Do not power the machine before completing wiring and verifying all connections.

Tip

Use an old laptop running Klipper as an alternative to a Raspberry Pi if needed.

docs.title: X+Z Carriage Plate Assembly
docs.text: This chapter covers assembling the X+Z carriage, mounting motors, bearings, and spacers.

docs.title: 1. Parts Required
docs.text: Qty Item Source Notes 4pc M3x8 BHSC Ender3 2pc M3x10 Buy 10pc M5x16 BHSC Buy 2pc M5x8 BHSC Ender3 4pc M3x40 Ender3 4pc M5x45 SHSC Ender3 Remove shims 1pc M5x65 BHSC Buy 12pc M3x8 SHSC Buy 2pc M3x16 BHSC Ender3 9pc Aluminum Spacers Ender3 1pc 28.35mm Printed Spacer Printed 5pc V-Wheels Ender3 4pc 32.60mm Printed Spacer Printed 8pc M3x10 BHSC Ender3

docs.title: 2. Safety Notes
docs.text:
Warning

Do not press in the endstops until wiring is complete. Press-fit endstops can be very difficult to remove if installed too early.

docs.title: 3. Assemble the X+Z Carriage
docs.text:
1. Cut Ender3 2020 extrusions to 150mm x2.
  2. Tap M5 threads in both ends of the 150mm extrusions.
  3. Press in bearings if not already done.
  4. Loosely attach:
  5. Z motor coupler
  6. X motor pulley (20T)
  7. Attach both motors to their plates.
  8. Assemble the 2020 extrusions as square and parallel as possible.
  9. Mount the X motor carriage to the ZX carriage with wheels and spacers.

Tip

Ender3 aluminum spacers are 8.35mm. If using 8mm spacers from another source, use the printed lower middle spacer to maintain alignment.

  1. Attach the Z bottom bearing plate and Z motor plate.
  2. Mount the MGN12 rails with carriages to the 2020 extrusions:
  3. Use a printed MGN12->2020 alignment tool to ensure straight rails.
  4. Rails can slide into cutouts if slightly too long (max 4mm overhang).
  5. Attach Z leadscrew nut to Z carriage.
  6. Mount Z carriage to MGN12H carriages:
    • Slide up and down to check for binding. Adjust rails if necessary.
  7. Cut leadscrew to size (~180mm), double-check with your setup.
  8. Screw leadscrew from bottom bearing to Z motor coupler and secure.
  9. Slide the X+Z assembly on the previously cut X extrusion.

docs.title: Z Carriage Assembly
docs.text: This chapter focuses on the Z-axis carriage, bearings, and coupler alignment.

docs.title: 1. Parts Required
docs.text: Qty Item Source Notes 1pc Z Carriage Plate Printed Heatset inserts required 2pc M5 Locknuts Buy Press-fit into plate 2pc M5x16 BHSC Buy Z-axis carriage attachment 1pc Leadscrew Nut Buy M5 threaded for Z 2pc MGN12H Carriages Buy For smooth linear motion

docs.title: 2. Installation Steps
docs.text:
1. Insert heatset inserts into Z carriage plate.
  2. Press-fit M5 locknuts into their designated holes.
  3. Attach MGN12H carriages to the 2020 extrusions if not already done.
  4. Slide Z carriage onto MGN12H carriages.
  5. Attach leadscrew nut to Z carriage.
  6. Loosely attach coupler to Z motor shaft.
  7. Insert leadscrew from bottom bearing to coupler:
  8. Ensure smooth rotation by hand.
  9. Adjust alignment if carriage binds on rails.

docs.title: 3. Tips & Tricks
docs.text:
Tip

Test the Z-axis movement manually before wiring the motors. Smooth motion now saves time troubleshooting later.

Warning

Avoid forcing the leadscrew; misalignment can strip threads in the coupler.
